sentences.text: I'll introduce everybody.
Boon is snoring really loud right under me.
Holy….
Hey guys!
Bye!
Hi!
Good morning.
Oh, you know, just dealing with meta forever and always.
That's, look, look at Andre shaking his head, he's like, yeah.
Yep.
That's too.

Awful, and to be honest, like, we… I mean, we've always dealt with Meta, so, like, whatever, but I feel like the last 3 days have just been horrific, and not just with you guys, but with a lot of our other third-party organic social media vendors that we partner with, so it's just… It's been a lot.
We're kind of working behind the scenes with some of our IT folks, we're working with some of our friends at other organizations, trying to figure out, like.
what's our new standard in terms of the two-factor authentication and things like that?
I know… I know that's not what this call is about, but kind of.
So, yeah.
I mean, it's just… the ever… the consensus is just that Meta's difficult in every aspect, basically, right?
Like, I really think if they just took a step back and treated Instagram exactly like they treated Facebook, we would not be in this situation.
I do not understand that.
For the lifetime.
with me.
Like, it's….
Weird.
It's very frustrating.
Bizarre.
Well, thank you guys for hopping on a call.
Paul is actually on another… in a meeting with our new SVP, so he's unable to join.
He might join a little bit later, but, I feel like I can kind of handle it for… from here.
I did log into the dashboard, and… I attempted to add myself as a user, just like as a backup for Paul, but I was unable to, so I went… long story short, I went ahead and deleted Michelle Wajaja.
She does not work for our organization anymore, so I've removed her.
Walk me through what other users you guys had in this dashboard.
Tiffany.
Are you able to.
Yes.
Andre?
No, not currently, so I have to admit, that's something we're… we're in the process of adding history tracking to that.
Awesome.
So, but as of yesterday, sorry, let me just check my notes, the…
Sorry, it's in Slack.
We were down to 2 users, who are… neither of them are in there anymore.
I think you removed them.
It sounds like you removed them this morning.
And as part of that transition, Paul got removed, which… is not good.
So… and that was, what caused our issue, with Post Not going out on Tuesday, because, like, we connect to Meta as.
Yep.

you know, through Paul's authentication, and since he was no longer in the Maven product, that meant we couldn't connect to Meta anymore, so that's kind of the underlying, cause of what happened on Tuesday.
Oh wait, I have a question for you, Jess.
Sorry, not to interrupt you, but there were two users.
There was marketing, and then there was, like….
Michelle Wajaja?
Okay, so you removed Michelle Wajaja.
Did you remove marketing as well?
I did not.
Marketing and Paul are still in here, so I have the dashboard open right here, and they are still in there.

I don't know if they were added yesterday, I'm unsure if Paul maybe went back through and added us again, but… Paul is really the only one that logs into this dashboard, so I don't know what occurred.
It could have just been a glitch, it could have been… somebody got Paul's login.
I mean, everybody has this login, so… but again, I don't think… would actively go against us to remove all the users, but Paul was in there, marketing is in there.
I do think we can add me in there as a backup if this ever occurs again.
I'm a primary admin on our Facebook account, so I'll be able to… I still have the same access as Paul, so we could add me if we can.
When you're… when you guys are implementing new properties, is it you or Paul that's giving us access via the backend?
Purple.
It's Paul, it is Paul, okay, because I was.
Gonna say.

can make you the pro… like, I don't know, Andre, if there even has to be a primary, if they both can have access to do the same thing so that she can also grant access, I don't know if she ever needed to, or does it have to be one person that does the granting?
It's a good question, it doesn't have to be, but I think it's best practice.
Okay.
For example.
So, Jess, Andre's our product, lead, so I should have introduced him at the beginning.
Oh, yeah.
Awesome!
Thank you.
He is the one that's, like, building the admin from the back end.
Anna is our director over customer success.

You obviously know Paige and myself, but yeah, so apologies for not introducing, but that's… so that's why I'm leaning on him a little bit, because he's the one that's, like, looking in the back end, reading all the code, and putting it in human terms for us over here, so….
He has to deal with Meta, got it.
Yes, I was gonna say, we've been in the thick of… Meta's favorite thing is that nothing works like the documentation says, that it does.

that's the fun we've also been dealing with lately, and we're just trying to, you know, we're really focused on getting the reliability of this improved as best we can, given the constraints of Meta's seemingly capricious behavior with some of this stuff, you know?
Super fun, super fun.
If… so we… I'm fine with that as our best practice, leading Paul is the kind of adder-approver of all things.
I do think, like, say, for instance, he's going out of town for a week, or two weeks, or whatever, I do think it's helpful to have one more person on there.
So if we can add me as another back-end user, that would be awesome.
If not, we can figure it out, too.

And then, Andre, they… like, they can… obviously, they have other marketing leads, and we can ask Paul if, like, who do you want to have access to the backend, or… because I… at one point, Paul, like, basically wanted to just use his login universally, and maybe… I don't know, maybe?
So.
Isn't.
We're doing that… we're doing that now through the marketing email, so we all have access to the marketing email.

I think it's just… I love to have individual users, but it sounds like you guys don't have that history tracking set up quite yet, but when we get to that point, I would love to have individual users so that we can go see who deleted all of our users on Tuesday.
Yeah, because we can have individual users, but we just don't have the tracking for it yet, so if you feel more comfortable.
Yes.

with one for now, and then once we have the tracking, we can give each person hope and everybody their own login, and we can even tailor what they can view from, like, a post, like, what properties they can view, or what their capabilities.
Okay.
from the back end.
But… Okay.

Yeah, it's a little bit of a mystery of, like, how these all got deleted, and I hope to avoid that in the future by having a tracking system so we would be able to go in and say, hey, like, we know exactly how this happened, but I think.
No.
to Andre's best point, we can do best practice would be Paul is your main, and then you're also available to do that if needed.
Yep.
And then mark… the marketing one is universal, and then at the… once we do get to a point where we can track it, then we can give individual logins and customize them as you want.
Okay, that's perfect.
Okay, regarding the issue from Tuesday, can you guys confirm that you guys are all set in terms of, like, everything's back to normal, or are we kind of still waiting on anything from us?
So, from what I can see, it looks like Paul… I just wanted to confirm with you, it looks like Paul, you know, he added his account back in, and then he authenticated.
like, said, hey, I want to connect to the Meta account.
That's what I'm seeing on my end.
I just want to confirm with you that.
That's what he did, because we're going to go and reassociate all the pages, And then we should be good to go, but I just want to confirm that that was the, … That was what he did.

I don't know what Paul does on a day-to-day basis, but let me confirm with him, like, as soon as he gets out of this meeting, he's just right next door to me, so let me confirm with him, and I can send you guys a follow-up email just to check that box.

Yeah, and I'm kind of, like, maybe being extra cautious, because that's… I'm fairly confident that's what happened, that's what we're seeing on our end, but I just want to make sure that that was the process that he went through, because we are all of the Facebook pages, available, in our system now.
So we're just gonna go and remap everything.
So that should mean that this afternoon, the outstanding post… I mean, the posts that are scheduled to go out at 3 p.
m.
Central Time, they go out across all the, properties.
That we have access to.
And I don't… I will… one thing we'll do also this morning is, I'm not familiar with all the list of properties, but we'll double-check and make sure.
Okay.
That is covering everything, that y'all have set up.
Okay, we also have an internal tracker, so if you guys need confirmation of what those properties are, just let me know, okay?
Yeah.
Perfect.
Awesome.
Okay, can you guys walk me through, kind of… so, as you guys know, Instagram is going through this two-factor authentication required at all… on all pages.
It's kind of a slow burn in this situation, just because it's happening sporadically.
How are you guys… How do you guys post to Instagram?
Is it through Meta?

Yes, yeah, so we connect to the Meta Business Suite, and then, so we, we don't require, like, access to your two-factor, it's just that, whoever… now, I think the thing that will, will come up is that, If you activate two-factor authentication.
on a page, generally, and I don't know for sure that Meta does this, but generally the way things work is, like, when you do that, things become disconnected, because that's kind of best practice.

And so, I think what we'll want to do is just stay kind of in sync with you, and, like, maybe one thing that'd be helpful is, like, hey, just send us an email, you know, on the next page that you turn two-factor authentication on, and we'll just go through and make sure That it didn't affect anything about our access.
And there is a chance that we may need to, like, once that person does that, they may need to re-authenticate those pages, but I'm hoping that's not the case.
But I think we'll just want to keep an eye on that in case it doesn't behave as we're expecting.

Okay, Andre, I did have to, do… it required me to set up the two-factor authentication for Maple District Lofts, and I believe they are active with y'all's platform, so if you guys wouldn't mind taking a peek at that one, that one, I couldn't even reset the password without enabling it, so….
Okay.
it… yeah, it was a whole thing.
So just let me know if there are any issues with air.
When did you do that, do you know?
Tuesday was my meta day from hell, so yeah, Tuesday.
Okay, Tuesday.
Alright, so we'll take a look at that, ….
Okay.
And, …
Yeah, well, so we have a post scheduled for them today.

I would say that's probably the one that's potentially most at risk of not going out, so I'll keep a special eye on that one and see if there are any errors that pop up there, and that'll give us insight into… because especially if you change the password, which it sounds like you're required to do, that can mean that reauthentication is required on the, on the meta side.
And could I just maybe ask you, do y'all have any other providers that are integrating with your social pages?
Yes, we have a few.
So, we partner with, Social Capture, who is another one that we do, organic and paid, through, Beacon, which is our primary PPC provider.
They also do paid social for us.
Okay.

I think that's it, other than maybe some of our LiveCore properties utilize AdTaxi, like, they partner with Ad Taxi on the back end, but I'm gonna be completely honest, I had a call with Invita, and they're going through the same issues, so… Okay.

I was gonna ask if, you know, in some cases, especially with agencies, they'll ask you to set up your meta pages in a slightly different way, where you kind of, like, delegate permissions, or kind of, like, make the… do you have any partners that do that?
… It's been a long time since I set up our partners, so, ….
Honestly.
Nothing unique.
Yeah, I think we're doing things the best way possible.
Okay.
And I wanted to, like, hey, is anybody doing this in a way that isn't causing you issues?
And it sounds like, no, there's issues with everybody.
Issues across the board, yep, issues across.
There isn't a single… I mean, even with Sochi, so we utilize Sochi for our, social media planning content manager, and it… I mean, consistent issues.

So, just for visibility with you guys, I have calls over the next week or so with every single social vendor to kind of, like, poke their brains and figure out what's going on, but… Yeah, issues across the board.
Okay.

Well, I mean, you know, we know this is painful, and we're doing this… I'm, like, doing a lot of research and testing about what are ways to improve it, but if anybody is finding solutions that are working better for y'all… I'll share.
We're super excited to implement them.
Yeah.
And we want to jump on that as soon as possible.
Obviously, something we're working on and talking with Meta about, but, like, you know, anything you hear, definitely let me know.
Yep.
I mean, I don't even know if our Facebook backend is even set up the way it should be, to be completely honest.
This was set up probably 8 years ago, before my time.
It was an acquired… piece of tech that I got, so it pretty messy on the back end.

I mean, up until 4 years ago, we were still using one individual login, and I mean, so we just started adding everybody as individual users on the back end, so it is still very much a work in progress, and if we need to update our backend for anything, just let us know.
For sure.
And I will say, of all the, like, you're not alone in that, if that makes you feel better.
I don't think I've talked to any customer who's like, we got this.
Very well sorted, it's perfect, especially with acquisitions and dispositions.
It's just so… it's so complicated to, to handle that aspect of things.
Yeah.
Yep.
Well, perfect.
I appreciate you guys.
I'll confirm with Paul that that's what he did on the back end, authenticated everything.
Keep me updated on Maple District Lofts, I'll keep an eye on that, too.
Was there anything else I owe you guys?
I'll keep you updated on if I hear anything from any other partners, regarding social media, but… Anything else that you guys need from me?
I was just gonna ask, … I don't know if this really falls into your wheelhouse or not, Jess, but I can ask Paul or ping him today.

We have a lot of properties that have been added to the Smartsheet for implementation, and we're just… some Google and social access for them, so we're hoping to get as much of them implemented as we can before the end of the month.

So if there's any way that he can pop in that Smartsheet and look at where we still need… There's been a couple that were indicated that it was turned on, but we were just not seeing it on our end, so I don't know if something… fluky happened.
I emailed Alan, but if you could maybe just ask him, that would be awesome.
Helping them, yep, sounds perfect.
We have a few other team members that helped set up that Smartsheet, so there's multiple people submitting content, so I will confirm.
Oh, I do have a question for you guys, but yes, I'll confirm on that one.
So, I was looking in our dashboard, and I noticed that there are a lot of hashtags, and I know I've recently come across, kind of.
Some literature about minimizing the amount of hashtags we're using on different sources.
So, like, LinkedIn you can use too, like, and so forth.

So, I was wondering if you guys have any… like, data that shows, hey, don't use 12 hashtags, maybe use 4 hashtags, or anything like that, because I'm looking at the one for Towns at Blake Farm, … It's, like, the third scheduled post, and it's got, like, 15 hashtags.
So I'm just wondering, maybe we minimize the amount, or maybe there's a science behind it that you guys can share.
Yes, that's a good question.
Now, that may be because of… I wonder… so….
We probably submitted all of them.
Yeah, yeah.
So, I was just talking to a customer about this yesterday, and I think the best we have right now, at least for me, is, like, our intuitions, which is 15's probably too many, you know?
Yeah.
I think… and I mostly go by what I see, you know, which is, like.
2 to 3, you know, like, more in that range.
Yeah.

And, I think the other thing is, and we were talking to a customer about this yesterday, is kind of, like, making them… … you know, if the idea of hashtags is that you increase the chance that it'll show up, like, in a Discover search or something.
And like that.
Generally, my guidance is to focus on the hashtags that are like, the Goldilocks hashtags, I suppose.
They're….
Yeah.
that there might be some search volume, but they're specific enough that you're not gonna get, like, drowned out, so I think, like.
Yep.

in the case of, like, towns at Blake Farms, I think, like, things like, townhomes and luxury homes are less likely to be, you know, beneficial, and, like, I think, … things that are maybe more regional, might be, you know, more beneficial, but I will say I'm doing some research on this right now to try to see how that, affects search, because one thing that's not easy to get a peek into is, how much does Meta How much do they take your geographic location as, like, an additional search filter when you are looking… when you're searching?
Yep.
hashtag, you know, so we're doing some research on that right now.
Okay.

So yeah, I think probably our best bet would be maybe to clean up the list of hashtags that we've configured in the platform, because that, for instance, on that post, the first ones, like, in the main section.
Yeah.
that the AI came up with, and then the following ones are the ones that are defined in the backend.
Yeah.
And so, I think maybe we should work together.
So let me do a little bit of research, and then….
Okay, fine.
kind of sharpen our guidance a little bit, but yeah, I think we should probably work together to pare down that list of defined hashtags.
That'd be awesome.

I mean, I don't even think that's needed anymore.
I know we added the Managed by Bell Partners and We Care We Deliver across.
but I'm almost thinking, do we need the week care we deliver across the board?
So, let me clean up that list a little bit, and let me know what you guys find, and I'll update our internal training.
Okay, yeah, that sounds great.
And then one.
Question.
for you, is would you like us to, add guidance to our AI to tell it not to come up with any of its own, like, hashtags that may be relevant to the post?
Because then, you know, we might have three, and then if you have four, I'm just curious how you think about that.
So… I almost prefer AI hashtags to be in the body of the sentence.
Yeah.
So it's like, with the content you're writing, in the middle of the sentence, you're writing hashtag something.
So, like, for the first example on the screen, Kitchen Islands at Arabelle City Center, Cook Dime Wine to Entertain, like, I would have preferred to put kitchen goals in the… Middle of the syntax.
Hmm.
Okay.
But I don't know if you guys can adjust that.
I, … but I hear you.
I like the idea of having AI hashtags.
I think it ties into what y'all are doing, but I would like it to be more… authentic and conversational.
Yeah, that's a great point of feedback.
I will say that that's not currently, a capability of this system, like, but, I think it's a great idea, so let me go see what we can cook up, with regards to that.
Yeah, yeah, because I do think it would make I think, like, having the hashtag as part of the sentence is just a little bit more human.
Yeah.
And maybe we just test it.
If you guys get to that point, and you guys want to test it with any of our Bell assets, just let me know.

Yes, yeah, and this is getting a little bit in the guts of things, but we've recently really improved our ability to, like, give you… give individual users, like, little slight test versions of things, so that's a new capability that we're working on, and this is exactly the sort of circumstance I was hoping it would help.
Yeah.
You know, so, let me go see what we can do on that, and see if we can, try to come up with a version that will integrate the hashtags, like, within the sentence, and see how that looks.
Okay, awesome.
Do you guys have a standing call that I can join, by chance?
Yeah, we do.
I think a lot of our conversations could be, like, we could continue them on a weekly or biweekly basis, I'm unsure how you guys are… what you're.
Yeah, we meet bi-weekly with Paul, so if you want us to.
Yeah, exactly.
Totally, yeah.
Yeah, that'd be awesome.
Yeah, you can just forward that to me.
What days are those on?
Do you happen to know.
We're always on Fridays at 11am, so our next one's next week.
Okay, that's perfect.
Oh wait, let's see, actually, I take that back.
Yeah, next week.
So today, this week is an off week, but….
Okay, awesome.
Well, maybe by then we'll have some more… or more things on the back end, so keep me updated.
Awesome.
Thank you so much for hopping on, Claren.
Awesome, speaking on.
Moving forward.
You too.
Thank you, nice to meet you.
time.
